item.title: [ Mod ] KM Style Michiru Kagemori (Rochelle) with L4D Styled Icons
item.description:
View Article
Media
Media gallery
1 / 4
This is a personal custom-edit mod of 7EMP3ST’s KM style Michiru Kagemori (Rochelle) mod. It alters the VGUI/HUD icons (the Lobby, Panel, and Incap icons) to a cleaner L4D-styled look. It also keeps the model intact/included but slightly edited on the textures of the jacket, which removes the gray lines from the original model since it looked out of place. I made this mod because the original portraits that come with this model, while they are not bad by any stretch, they doesn't match the portraits that come with the majority of skin mods from other modders. Original Mod: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?l=spanish&id=3084780837
item.content:encoded:
View Article
Media
Media gallery
1 / 4
This is a personal custom-edit mod of 7EMP3ST’s KM style Michiru Kagemori (Rochelle) mod. It alters the VGUI/HUD icons (the Lobby, Panel, and Incap icons) to a cleaner L4D-styled look. It also keeps the model intact/included but slightly edited on the textures of the jacket, which removes the gray lines from the original model since it looked out of place. I made this mod because the original portraits that come with this model, while they are not bad by any stretch, they doesn't match the portraits that come with the majority of skin mods from other modders. Original Mod: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?l=spanish&id=3084780837

item.title: [ Mod ] Solum Aesthetics
item.description:
View Article
Media
Media gallery
1 / 8
This addon covers both L4D1 and L4D2 campaigns. This uses the original Team Silent Aesthetics as a base and covers L4D2 Campaigns. I tried to aim for a 'realistic' look with mostly clear and cloudy skies, sprinkled in some fog that looks the best on Swamp Fever, there are no stars in the sky, figured it'd feel lonelier that way. None of the original skyboxes/color corrections were touched and therefore every visual aspect from the original addon is the same in this one. All of the skyboxes were sampled from gamebanana, color corrections made by me and sampled from various sources (Silent Hill franchise, Backrooms wiki, r/liminalspace images). The fog script was adjusted and edited to enable it on L4D2 Campaigns. (Special thanks to yorver for giving me permission to do so.) All graffiti has been removed to fit SOLUM. You're gonna have to see The Passing for yourself. This addon is not finished, I am planning to make my own custom materials. Credits: Darker L4D2 World Textures by Farty Freak Team Silent Aesthetics by yoru and Retro Customizable Fog Script by ChimiChamo
item.content:encoded:
View Article
Media
Media gallery
1 / 8
This addon covers both L4D1 and L4D2 campaigns. This uses the original Team Silent Aesthetics as a base and covers L4D2 Campaigns. I tried to aim for a 'realistic' look with mostly clear and cloudy skies, sprinkled in some fog that looks the best on Swamp Fever, there are no stars in the sky, figured it'd feel lonelier that way. None of the original skyboxes/color corrections were touched and therefore every visual aspect from the original addon is the same in this one. All of the skyboxes were sampled from gamebanana, color corrections made by me and sampled from various sources (Silent Hill franchise, Backrooms wiki, r/liminalspace images). The fog script was adjusted and edited to enable it on L4D2 Campaigns. (Special thanks to yorver for giving me permission to do so.) All graffiti has been removed to fit SOLUM. You're gonna have to see The Passing for yourself. This addon is not finished, I am planning to make my own custom materials. Credits: Darker L4D2 World Textures by Farty Freak Team Silent Aesthetics by yoru and Retro Customizable Fog Script by ChimiChamo

item.title: [ Mod ] Yoshi.exe Jumpscare Incoming Horde Sound Effect
item.description:
View Article
Media
Media gallery
1 / 1
(TW: Yoshi.exe (Not Interested)) Here's the mod I made for the 2009 video game Left 4 Dead 2 . :) This Jumpscare Sound Effect replaces the Incoming Horde Sound Effect. It might be really loud. Enjoy! Credit: Yoshi.exe - Skashi
item.content:encoded:
View Article
Media
Media gallery
1 / 1
